definitions: Fraud or gross negligence of a ship's master or crew at the expense of the ship's owner or cargo owners.
The offense of frequently exciting and stirring up lawsuits and quarrels.
In feudal law, the simoniacal purchase of ecclesiastical preferment.
examples: The insurance policy covered losses due to barratry.
He was accused of barratry for instigating numerous frivolous lawsuits.
Historically, barratry was a concern in maritime trade.
